item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, this SCP, is a good SCP. No improvements needed. Just how a typical SCP is.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I know what's missing: what does the divide between the disappeared body part and the extant body part looks like? Does it look like that black stuff that 529 has? Or does it look like if one just chopped off the body part, except the blood & guts are just kinda suspended there, going through a transparent portal to the missing body part?

Another thing that I wonder: what happens if they attach a tracking device to a D-Class & have them touch SCP-1593? Would the tracking device just fall off if them, or would it go with them, but they can't find a signal? I personally think it's the former.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i love it! i wonder if theyd try and get someone to touch it with theyre eye to try and test if they see whats happening.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
i like this one, short simple and i dont think ive read a skip like this one. Pluss it was refreshing to see a D-Class with a different (and quite rational when you think about it) attitude.

+1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really hope we were merciful enough to shoot the poor son of a bitch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is freaky and very interesting. +1 for me. I really like the concept and execution. Though the writing could be a bit more clearer and detailed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing is fine, but this one's a little too blatantly horror-movie-like for me to feel sufficiently creeped out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really cute to read. Imagining everything feels like flesh, and the sensation of bones tugging at your intestines, pulling them away somewhere far far away into some vast plane of flesh and corpses…

Mmmm….

Adorable. Upvoted with glee.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm remaining neutral. I don't dislike this article, but it isn't interesting to me either. I don't know why, but as I was reading this I never thought anything like, "Oh, I like this touch" or "Yeah, that's creepy".

In short, I'm just not feeling it now Mr. Krabs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one doesnt do much for me. I would be interested to know what happens if they touch the head of a D class with it (although this might be beyond the criteria of the challenge if vision is affected). No vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done. My only quibble is the phrase "finger bone" in the description. In a summary report like this, I would think there would be a little more about which type of finger bone (distal, middle or proximal phalanx or metacarpal), since they are all different (see here). Measurements of the bone and an approximate age determination would fill in a blank for me. This is a minor point, however.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Drewbear approves. +1

(omg someone actually made me something for my birthday omg omg!!!)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the interview log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the mystery behind this.

Here, take this +1. Would upvote again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exciting and unnerving.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a good, simple, disturbing SCP. I like to imagine that it's related to SCP-1704 and SCP-792.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Simple, interesting, thought-provoking, creepy, but not narmy.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The part of the individual's body which first made contact with SCP-1593 will begin to disappear at this point. Despite it's absence, the affected individual will suffer no physical discomfort, as blood will flow as if the affected region was still present and the individual will report that they are still able to feel it. In cases where the absence of certain parts of the body would result in other parts not being able to support themselves, these parts of the body will float in place and still be usable by the individual, even if the body part is completely unconnected to the rest of the body.

This paragraph feels…wrong somehow. Like a critical piece of information is missing or presented backward. It's hard to describe.

I like the effect but… Something about the writing voice just doesn't agree with my sensibilities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like how the D-Class is portrayed, too often they are just freaking out, he's remarkably calm despite the fact he is being pulled into an underground cavern filled with dead bodies. (I mean, who wouldn't be? :P)

In all seriousness though, I like this one. :P +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is a good one. Maybe some details could be added to increase the fear factor, so to speak. But overall, I can't really find anything about it that I don't like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Write a SCP that messes with or primarily includes a physical sense OTHER than vision and hearing.

For Drewbear's birthday :3
